summary:
Post-operative numbness near the ear after parotid surgery most probably results from damage to the auriculotemporal nerve as it supplies sensory function to these areas; thus, option B correctly identifies potential nerve involvement leading to such symptoms. Agent1 concluded based on known anatomical relationships between nerves involved during surgeries like parotidectomies and their specific functions within those zones affected clinically here.
